summary: End-to-end testing of the new Admin Visual Results Bracket at /admin/bracket. Backend pytest suite (11 tests) passes 11/11 — covering admin auth, /bracket/meta (12 official groups, Group A=México/Sudáfrica/Corea del Sur/Chequia, pichichi candidates), PUT/GET /api/bracket/admin/results full-payload persistence (group_positions, best_thirds, r32/r16/qf/sf_winners, third_place_winner, champion, runner_up, semi_finalists, top_scorer, final_score_home/away, mexico_to_quarters), partial-save support, AND a full pro-mode scoring proof: POST /api/bracket/submit (mode='pro') with a perfect bracket → PUT matching official results → score awarded (≥200 with r32+2*16, r16+3*8, qf+5*4, sf+8*2, group_positions +48, best_thirds +16, third_place +5, plus quick bonuses) and appears on /api/bracket/leaderboard and /api/bracket/admin/predictions with the correct score. Frontend: super-admin login lands on /super, /admin/bracket renders admin-results-bracket with the 3 step tabs, group cards show official teams, reorder buttons (group-A-down-0) actually swap teams, save button triggers PUT and a 'Resultados guardados — puntajes recalculados' success toast, and the participants table reloads with the new scores. KNOWN ISSUE (carried from iter 7): the visual bracket tree from QuinielaBracket renders both desktop and mobile DOM nodes with identical testids, so rapid sequential force-clicks don't reliably propagate winners up to the champion banner. The data flow is functionally correct (verified via backend pytest); admin's UI clicks would benefit from a single-tree refactor or namespaced testids.

ui_bugs.component: AdminResultsBracket reuses QuinielaBracket.StepBracket which renders BOTH desktop (.hidden.lg:block) and mobile (.lg:hidden) trees in DOM
ui_bugs.issue: Every bkt-* match testid (bkt-r32-{i}, bkt-r16-{i}, bkt-qf-{i}, bkt-sf-{i}, bkt-final-0 + -team-0/-team-1 children) exists twice in DOM. Rapid sequential Playwright clicks on the visible node intermittently fail to propagate r32→r16→qf→sf→champion. Manual user clicks (≥150ms spacing) are fine. Champion banner stayed at '?' during automated click-through despite 16 R32 clicks.
React console.error: 'An empty string ("") was passed to the src attribute.' Still reproducing — same as iter 7. Find the img with src='' and gate it.

action_items: Refactor StepBracket to render only ONE layout based on viewport (useMediaQuery) OR namespace testids per layout (bkt-r32-0-d / bkt-r32-0-m). This fixes both the admin and public brackets and makes automated testing deterministic.
Track down img with src='' (likely station logo / advertiser logo fallback) and conditionally omit the src attribute when empty.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminResultsBracket.jsx is clean (~290 lines) and correctly delegates rendering to QuinielaBracket's exported step components — good DRY. sanitize() cascade-clear logic mirrors the public bracket, verified consistent.

save() in AdminResultsBracket builds payload.semi_finalists from qf.filter(t !== champion && t !== runner_up). This works only if SF picks are made; for partial saves, semi_finalists will be empty (acceptable per design 'partial saves allowed').

Backend _score_prediction (server.py L2737) correctly applies +2/+3/+5/+8 round bonuses and uses _norm() for accent-insensitive matching — verified end-to-end via test_admin_predictions_contains_score (perfect bracket scored >= 200).

PUT /api/bracket/admin/results re-scores ALL predictions on every save (server.py L3178 _recalculate_all_scores). For large participant counts this is O(N) per save; acceptable now but consider Pub/Sub or queued recompute at scale.
/api/bracket/leaderboard returns {total, rows} (not a bare array) — be sure all frontend callers handle this shape. AdminBracket consumes /bracket/admin/predictions (bare array), so OK.

success_rate.backend: 100% (11/11 pytest cases passed; admin login, meta, GET/PUT results, partial save, full pro-mode scoring via leaderboard + admin/predictions)
success_rate.frontend: 90% (admin-results-bracket loads, all step tabs render, official groups correct, reorder works, save+toast+participants reload all verified; only the visual bracket click-propagation has the known duplicate-testid limitation)

test_credentials: Super Admin pzsuave007@gmail.com / MXmedia007 — verified working; lands on /super.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created 1 prediction per pytest run with email TEST_<uuid>@example.com (mode='pro'). After the run, official results in db.bracket_results were set first to a 'full perfect' bracket, then overwritten by the TestPartialAdminSave test to a partial state (champion='México', everything else empty). Main agent may want to (a) restore official results to a sensible default before production, (b) delete TEST_* rows from db.bracket_predictions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression test: /app/backend/tests/test_bracket_admin_results.py — run with `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://la-campeona-radio.preview.emergentagent.com pytest backend/tests/test_bracket_admin_results.py -v`. All 11 cases must pass. Useful admin testids: admin-results-bracket, admin-bracket-step-{groups,thirds,bracket}, admin-bracket-save, admin-bracket-save-bottom (only visible on last step), admin-bracket-reset, admin-bracket-prev, admin-bracket-next, group-{A..L}, group-{gid}-up-{idx}, group-{gid}-down-{idx}, third-{teamName}, bkt-r32-{0..15}/r16/qf/sf/final each with -team-0/-team-1, bracket-champion-banner, final-score-home, final-score-away, final-pichichi, third-pick-{team}, mxqf-true/false, admin-participants-table. When automating the visual tree, use bounding_box width>0 AND wait ≥150ms between sequential pickWinner clicks because the desktop and mobile trees both mount. POST /api/bracket/submit returns {id, edit_token, mode, score}. /api/bracket/leaderboard returns {total, rows: [...]} (NOT bare array). For partial saves (admin doesn't need to fill all rounds) the endpoint accepts BracketOfficialResults with empty arrays — verified.
rca_of_the_issue:
Duplicate testid in StepBracket: the component renders
desktop tree
AND
mobile stack
unconditionally. Tailwind only toggles CSS display — both DOM subtrees remain mounted with identical data-testid attributes. When Playwright queries by testid it gets count=2; rapid force-clicks may dispatch to the offscreen copy (which still calls pickWinner via React but on a stale closure since the visible copy didn't receive the click). The functional pickWinner+sanitize chain is correct (proven by backend perfect-bracket scoring test scoring >= 200). Mitigation: render only the matching branch via useMediaQuery('(min-width: 1024px)') OR rename testids per branch.
